title: diploblechnum neglectum
intro: Diploblechnum neglectum, also known as the neglected strap fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Diploblechnum neglectum include its anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing swelling and pain, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, teas, and decoctions, making it a popular choice for natural remedies.
